description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly reddish-brown coloration. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring a mix of flat and jagged faces, with some lighter beige and white patches scattered across its exterior. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or significant cracks, and has a weathered look typical of natural stone found outdoors. Some darker stains and mineral deposits are visible, adding to its naturalistic appearance. The coloration and texture suggest it could be sandstone or a similar sedimentary rock, commonly found in urban landscaping or as a natural barrier. Its size and mass would make it a substantial obstacle in an urban environment, suitable for use as a decorative or functional barrier.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown rock with rough, angular surfaces and lighter patches, showing natural weathering and mineral stains.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's angular top and a prominent, flat face with reddish-brown and white coloration.
description_view_1: The rock appears broader and flatter from this angle, with more visible beige and lighter patches on its surface.
description_view_2: This side shows a more rounded, less angular profile, with a mix of reddish and beige tones and some dark mineral stains.
description_view_3: The rock looks elongated and narrow from this perspective, with a rough, uneven surface and a blend of dark and light patches.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
